_note: 2026-07-23 batch (batch 1 of a seminaries/midrashot-only research pass, scoped strictly to post-high-school women's institutions -- no yeshivot, mechinot, other gap-year programs, or university programs included even when found along the way). Primary source: Touro Lander College for Women's 'Participating Seminaries' list (lcw.touro.edu, dated Feb 2025), which named 46 institutions -- cross-checked against a fresh dump of the DB's existing 52 seminaries/midrashot (identified via the 'Girls only' tag, manually filtered to exclude mechinot/gap-year/volunteer programs that also carry that tag). ~28 of the 46 were confirmed duplicates of already-listed programs under a different name/spelling/acronym; the rest were individually verified. Every signupUrl below returned a live page via WebFetch and/or curl on 2026-07-23; several plausible candidates (listed in the rejected section, not stored here) were dropped for lacking any independently verifiable official website -- common in this space, since some Charedi-network seminaries maintain minimal or no web presence beyond third-party directory listings. Tags used below are all pre-existing, matching the exact tag set already applied to structurally similar seminaries (Baer Miriam, Machon Sara Yerushalayim, Bnos Chava, Chemdas Bais Yaakov, etc.) rather than invented fresh.

seminaries_midrashot.name: Aish Gesher Women
seminaries_midrashot.description: A post-high-school seminary for young women within the Aish HaTorah network, combining foundational Jewish studies with an emphasis on personal and spiritual growth for students newer to intensive Torah study.
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Apply via the online application portal linked from the program's website.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Sibling program to the already-listed 'EYAHT - Aish HaTorah College for Women' and 'JEWEL for Women' within the same Aish network, but confirmed as a distinct program (own site, own admissions).

seminaries_midrashot.name: Jean Schottenstein Oryah Institute (JSOI)
seminaries_midrashot.description: A Chabad-Lubavitch seminary for young women using the teachings of Chassidus to develop leadership and a deep connection to Torah, including Torah classes, tours in Israel, and chesed/internship opportunities.
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Applications open December 21; deadline January 11. Interviews held in Crown Heights or via Zoom for out-of-town applicants.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Confirmed distinct from the already-listed Chabad women's seminaries (Machon Alte in Tzfat, Beit Chana Tzfat Chul Program) -- separate institution, separate location (Givat Ze'ev), also also known as the Oryah Seminary/Institute. Added 'Haredi/Ultra-Orthodox/Yeshivish' alongside 'chabad' to match the exact tag pairing on both existing Chabad seminaries (Machon Alte, Beit Chana Tzfat both carry both tags together).

seminaries_midrashot.name: Machon Bnos Yehuda (BYA)
seminaries_midrashot.description: A Bais Yaakov-style seminary, now in its 35th year, geared toward highly motivated Bais Yaakov high school graduates seeking a serious year of Torah study in Israel.
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Contact the seminary's US office for current application status.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: The site displayed 'registration closed, no longer accepting applications for next year' at time of research (2026-07-23) -- likely a normal seasonal admissions-cycle closure (the institution is a current Touro Israel Option participant as of Feb 2025) rather than the program having ended, but worth confirming before outreach. Tags corrected to the plain 5-tag baseline used by every other checked 'Bais Yaakov'-named seminary (Baer Miriam, Bnos Sarah, Bnos Chava, Rinas, MSY, Ateres Bnos Yerushalayim, Chemdas Bais Yaakov all use this exact set, none carry a Haredi/Ultra-Orthodox/Yeshivish tag).

seminaries_midrashot.name: Me'ohr Bais Yaakov
seminaries_midrashot.description: A Bais Yaakov seminary for post-high-school women seeking serious textual Torah study in a traditional framework, drawing students from a range of Bais Yaakov-affiliated backgrounds.
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Apply via the applicant portal linked from the main site.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: The live site (meohr.com) is presented primarily as an alumnae portal, but includes a current-year application intake -- no separate, cleaner admissions-facing URL was found. Tags corrected to the plain 5-tag baseline (see note on Machon Bnos Yehuda above for the sibling evidence).

seminaries_midrashot.name: Nachlas Bais Yaakov
seminaries_midrashot.description: A Bais Yaakov-style seminary for post-high-school women pursuing a year of Torah study and religious growth in Jerusalem.
seminaries_midrashot.cost: $32,500 tuition (excludes airfare and insurance); $7,900 non-refundable deposit
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Enrollment portal requires a prior acceptance letter; contact the Israel or US office to begin the application process.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: A separate, unrelated-looking domain (nachlasby.com, 'Educating, Engaging, Inspiring Every Child') surfaced during research -- appears to be a different, younger-children-oriented institution under a similar name; not used as the source here. Tags corrected to the plain 5-tag baseline (see note on Machon Bnos Yehuda above for the sibling evidence).

seminaries_midrashot.name: B'nos Avigail
seminaries_midrashot.description: A Bais Yaakov-style seminary for serious post-high-school students, operating on the Neve Yerushalayim campus under its own separate leadership and admissions.
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Complete the information form on the program's site to begin the application process.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Tags corrected to the plain 5-tag baseline (see note on Machon Bnos Yehuda above for the sibling evidence). Physically located on the Neve Yerushalayim campus (which hosts 11 schools/seminaries total, of which only 2 -- Machon Devorah and the Shalhevet Program -- were already listed) but run by its own separate directors (Rabbi Dovid Kass, Rebbetzin Tziporah Heller-Gottlieb), not a Neve Yerushalayim administrative division. No independent institutional domain was found; sourced from the co-director's own site, the best verified live page available.

seminaries_midrashot.name: Havineini Bais Yaakov
seminaries_midrashot.description: A Bais Yaakov seminary combining rigorous textual study (Chovos Halevovos, Tanach, Halacha, Tefilla) with extracurricular enrichment, offering both a first-year (Shana Alef) and second-year (Shana Bais) track.
seminaries_midrashot.durationText: 1 year (Shana Alef), with a Shana Bais option
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Submit the application form and application letter, both available on the website; deadline is typically mid-December.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Tags corrected to the plain 5-tag baseline (see note on Machon Bnos Yehuda above for the sibling evidence).

seminaries_midrashot.name: Maalot Yerushalayim
seminaries_midrashot.organization: Maalot Educational Network (affiliated with Neve Yerushalayim)
seminaries_midrashot.description: A career-oriented post-secondary program for Bais Yaakov-aligned second-year (Shana Beis) students, combining Torah study with accredited academic coursework (speech-language pathology, psychology, education, business, computer science, and more) via a partnership with Gratz College.
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Apply via the 'Apply Now' link on the program's website.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Founded 1984 (formerly the Zaidner Institute); course credits recommended by ACE/NCCRS since founding. Targets students already in their second year (a madricha/mentor population), not first-year seminary students -- flagged since this is a somewhat different audience than most entries in this batch. Dropped 'Haredi/Ultra-Orthodox/Yeshivish' -- the plain Bais-Yaakov-style sibling pattern doesn't carry this tag (see Machon Bnos Yehuda note); the closest exception found (Darchei Binah, also a Touro/Daemen-partnered seminary) does carry it, but as a lone outlier against 7 other direct comparables it wasn't treated as the controlling pattern.

seminaries_midrashot.name: OU Israel ATID Program for Young Women
seminaries_midrashot.location: OU Israel Women's Hub, Rechavia, Jerusalem
seminaries_midrashot.description: A part-time evening midrasha for English-speaking women in their 20s already living in Israel, offering weekly classes in Tanach, Halacha, and Machshava alongside workshops, trips, and Shabbatonim, designed to fit around sherut leumi, university, or work schedules.
seminaries_midrashot.durationText: Weekly (Monday evenings); organized into mini-semesters, with single-session options
seminaries_midrashot.cost: 40 NIS per evening; 120 NIS for a winter semester
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Register online with name, email, WhatsApp number, and Aliyah year if applicable.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Structurally very different from every other entry in this batch: a part-time evening program for women already living/working/serving in Israel, not a full-time year-abroad seminary for recent high school graduates. Self-described as 'a part-time Midrasha experience,' which is why it's included here rather than excluded outright -- flagging clearly since it doesn't fit the typical mold and the user may prefer to exclude it. Added 'Spiritual growth' (its content is genuine structured Torah/Halacha/Machshava study, matching every other Torah-focused entry regardless of duration type) and 'Religious Zionism/Modern Orthodox' (OU Israel is a mainstream Modern Orthodox organization) -- both were missing from the initial draft.

seminaries_midrashot.name: Midreshet Olamot (The Midrasha)
seminaries_midrashot.description: An alternative, flexible-structure seminary for religious young women, combining Torah classes with a range of academic, vocational, and creative courses (art, music, cosmetology, languages) and options to complete a high school diploma, work, or volunteer.
seminaries_midrashot.durationText: Recommended 6-12 months, with an optional extension; first month is a trial period
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Apply via the online form or downloadable PDF application on the website.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Positions itself as a more flexible, lower-pressure alternative to a traditional seminary track (trial month, optional high-school-diploma completion, vocational/creative courses) -- worth noting for students for whom a standard seminary isn't the right fit.

seminaries_midrashot.name: Nishmas Bais Yaakov
seminaries_midrashot.description: A Bais Yaakov-style seminary for post-high-school women.
seminaries_midrashot.signupInstructions: Contact the office directly for application details.
seminaries_midrashot.adminNote: Tags corrected to the plain 5-tag baseline (see note on Machon Bnos Yehuda above for the sibling evidence). LOW CONFIDENCE on distinctness: Touro's participating-seminaries list separately names both 'B'not Torah Institute' (already listed as Sharfman's) AND 'B'not Torah Institute - Nishmas' as two line items, which could mean this is actually a track/division of the already-listed Sharfman's/Bnot Torah Institute rather than a fully independent institution -- despite having its own separate domain (nishmasby.org) with its own donation/tuition portal. Description above is thin (the site's content was mostly navigation/donation elements) since deeper program details weren't found. Recommend a closer look before importing.
